task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or code editor showing the agent opening, reading, or inspecting '~/pebl/data/wpt_data.csv' (e.g., using pandas read_csv, head, or cat commands).

task_completion.description: Agent writes logic to calculate optimal response rates per block and learning scores.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing logic to group data into 5 blocks of 20 trials, check if 'response' equals 'optimal_choice', and calculate the difference between Block 5 and Block 1.

task_completion.description: Agent explicitly handles the contaminated participant 'sub-999'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing explicit filtering or conditional logic to exclude 'sub-999' from group mean calculations and to add the '"excluded": true' flag for this participant in the output dictionary.

task_completion.description: Agent successfully generates the required JSON report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the creation of '~/pebl/analysis/wpt_report.json', or a printout/cat of the file showing the required structure with 'participants' (containing block_optimal_rates and learning_score) and 'group_learning_curve'.

task_completion.description: Agent successfully generates the learning curve line plot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer, terminal, or image viewer showing the creation of '~/pebl/analysis/wpt_learning_curve.png'. The code or image should indicate a line plot with Blocks 1-5 on the X-axis and Mean Optimal Response Rate on the Y-axis.

integrity.description: Agent used a script to compute the results rather than hardcoding fake values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a Python, R, or bash script containing actual data manipulation logic (e.g., pandas groupby, mean) rather than a script that just writes a hardcoded JSON string.

integrity.description: The plot was generated programmatically by the agent.
integrity.visual_evidence: Code editor showing plotting commands (e.g., matplotlib.pyplot, seaborn) saving to the exact required PNG path, rather than downloading or copying an existing image.

integrity.description: Outputs are saved to the correct target directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal commands or script paths explicitly showing files being written to '/home/ga/pebl/analysis/' or '~/pebl/analysis/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains exactly 100 trials per participant, which must be grouped into 5 blocks of 20 trials. Participant 'sub-999' is contaminated and must be explicitly excluded from group calculations and flagged in the JSON. The outputs must be saved exactly to /home/ga/pebl/analysis/wpt_report.json and /home/ga/pebl/analysis/wpt_learning_curve.png.
